smithery_ai.name: Smithery.ai
smithery_ai.method: Publish via CLI (smithery publish) or web UI. Supports BYOH (Bring Your Own Hosting) where Smithery Gateway proxies to the user's upstream server URL using Streamable HTTP transport.
smithery_ai.notes: Publishing flow requires completing the setup through the CLI or website after logging in with GitHub. The server must support Streamable HTTP transport. Smithery also offers hosted deployment options.

mcp_so.name: MCP.so
mcp_so.method: Direct web form submission on the /submit page. Users fill out fields and submit.
required_fields: Type (MCP Server or MCP Client) - radio button selection
Server Config (JSON textarea for mcpServers configuration)
mcp_so.notes: Form has a textarea for server config with placeholder showing a sample mcpServers JSON object (e.g., GitHub MCP server config with docker command, args, and env variables).

glama_ai.name: Glama.ai
glama_ai.method: GitHub repository-based submission. Maintainer authenticates via GitHub OAuth and Glama verifies write/admin access to the repository. Servers are auto-indexed from GitHub repositories and continuously synced.

authentication.type: GitHub OAuth (must have write/admin access to the repo being listed)

required_fields: Dockerfile (can be authored by maintainer or AI-inferred by Glama)
Server must pass automated build, introspection, and audit pipeline
glama_ai.notes: Servers are built from Dockerfiles. Glama runs protocol introspection (tools/list, resources/list, prompts/list). Security auditing is performed. Servers with failed builds are preserved but hidden from search. Connector maintainers provide sandbox credentials. Quality scores are assigned via tool-definition-quality-score.

clawhunt.name: ClawHunt
clawhunt.method: The site references /submit-tool and /api-docs in the footer navigation, but both pages return 404 errors. The site appears to be a concept/demo with placeholder tool data.
clawhunt.notes: Site describes itself as 'Product Hunt for AI Agents' with tools like SuperAPI, PaymentPro, DataStore as placeholders. Submission functionality is not currently available. The site may not be fully launched.

opentools.name: OpenTools
opentools.method: OpenTools focuses on providing 'One API to use any LLM with every MCP tool' - it is an API for consuming MCP tools, not a directory for submitting them. The /registry page lists MCP servers but has no visible submission mechanism. Documentation at /docs appears to be a Mintlify docs site that may require client-side rendering.
opentools.notes: The site is primarily an API gateway for LLM tool use rather than a submission directory. Users can sign up and get API access. GitHub org at github.com/opentools may contain SDKs and tools.
